Scenario.title: SolarWinds Compromise in Transportation Systems
Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Complex A sophisticated multi-stage attack targeting transportation infrastructure leverages supply chain compromises, phishing, and lateral movement to disrupt critical operations.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Open Source Intelligence (OSINT), File System, Internet-facing assets, Application Log, Windows Registry, Domain Name Registrar, OS Process, Windows Service, Process, Network Traffic, File
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Artificial Intelligence, Software Packing, General Access Method, Application Log Content (Mechanism), Windows Registry Key Creation (Mechanism), Pass the Hash, Process Injection, Keylogging, Reg.exe Utility, Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism), File Modification (Mechanism), Process Creation (Mechanism), Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker uses artificial intelligence tools to harvest publicly available data on transportation vendors' software ecosystems, identifying unpatched SCADA systems and network architecture weaknesses.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: A custom firmware update is crafted with malicious packing to evade detection, targeting industrial control systems used in transportation infrastructure.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: Maintenance staff are targeted via spoofed vendor emails containing malicious attachments, granting attackers initial access to internal networks.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Exploiting unpatched SCADA system logs, the attacker triggers execution of embedded malicious payloads to establish a foothold.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: A scheduled task is created in the registry to maintain persistent access during routine system updates.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.capec: CAPEC-461: Web Services API Signature Forgery Leveraging Hash Function Extension Weakness
stage.description: Credentials are harvested via pass-the-hash techniques to escalate privileges and access administrative control systems.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: Malicious code is injected into legitimate system processes to hide network activity and evade detection mechanisms.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: A keylogger is deployed within a Windows service to capture operator credentials for further access.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: Network traffic is analyzed using Reg.exe to map internal network topology and locate high-value targets like traffic management systems.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: Lateral movement occurs via remote services, exploiting predictable network traffic patterns to infiltrate interconnected transportation networks.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: Operational data, including route schedules and parameters, is exfiltrated by modifying and extracting files from compromised systems.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: Data is stealthily transferred via process creation tunnels to command-and-control servers, masking exfiltration activities.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Network traffic is manipulated to disable critical infrastructure components, creating physical safety risks and operational disruptions.

Scenario.summary: A complex attack leveraging AI-driven reconnaissance, supply chain exploitation, and lateral movement disrupts transportation systems, demonstrating the vulnerability of critical infrastructure to advanced persistent threats.
